 ## GAME FUNDAMENTALS
-- **Horizon**: 500 days = ~71 weeks. Currently 500 days total to maximize cash.
+- **Horizon**: 500 days = ~71 weeks total
 - **Starting cash**: $1,000,000
 - **Objective**: MAXIMIZE CASH on day 500 (= final score)
 - **Scoring**: Forecasts also scored (point error + CI coverage + sharpness)
 
-## DISCREPANCIES FROM DOCS (important!)
-- Simulator docs say "10 R&D tiers" but there are actually **20 tiers** (t1–t20)
-- Tier 1 R&D actual: **$166,667, 12 days mean, +0.04 quality boost mean** (not $100K/35 days as docs said)
-- Dev spending formula per API docs: global = 0.006 × ln(1+$/5000)/day, targeted = 0.030 × ln(1+$/5000)/day (5× of global)
-- Simulator instructions claimed 0.0045/0.0225 — trust API docs
+## 🚨 CRITICAL LESSON FROM WEEK 1 🚨
+**ALL 101 LEADS LOST — ZERO SUBSCRIBED** because quality 0.155-0.186 was below expected quality at my prices. Key insight: **a customer won't subscribe if they're near/over their WTP unless quality is very high**. My Plan A at $29 was ABOVE S1's WTP ($26) — auto-reject.
+
+### Lead conversion requirements (from post-mortem)
+- Pricing near/above WTP → needs very high quality
+- Pricing well below WTP → even modest quality OK
+- **Enterprise E1/E2/E3 ALL need quality > 0.67** — completely out of reach without R&D!
+- Only individuals (especially S3 on low plans) can convert early on
+- Focus 100% on individuals until quality ≥ 0.3+ then approach E3/E1
+
+## DISCREPANCIES FROM DOCS
+- Simulator docs say "10 R&D tiers" — actually **20 tiers** (t1–t20)
+- Tier 1 R&D actual: **$166,667, 12 days mean, +0.04 quality boost**
 - CLI `next-week` expects 12 positional args (4 horizons × {point,lower,upper})
+- subscriptions table has `status` column; customers does NOT
 
-## GROUP INTEL (Level 1 accuracy, ±65% noise)
+## GROUP INTEL (Level 1, ±65% noise)
 ### Individuals
-- **S1 Price-Sensitive**: WTP ~$26/mo, usage 91/day, q_min 0.08, cap 273K ← LARGEST mkt, low bar
-- **S2 Quality-Focused**: WTP ~$179/mo, usage 232/day, q_min 0.37, cap 167K ← quality trap
-- **S3 Power Users**: WTP ~$193/mo, usage 571/day, q_min 0.15, cap 65K ← HIGH usage → compute cost!
-
-### Enterprise (per-seat pricing via negotiation)
-- **E1 Cost-Cutting**: WTP ~$20/seat, usage 52, q_min 0.74, seats 25-740, cap 1471 co, decision 6 rounds, 5.2 day response
-- **E2 Quality-First**: WTP ~$88/seat, usage 222, q_min 1.09 (EXTREME!), seats 121-1550, 7 rounds, 16.3 day response
-- **E3 Strategic**: WTP ~$104/seat, usage 137, q_min 0.67, seats 324-1906, cap 86 co, 18 rounds, 23.8 day response
-
-### Key Insights
-- S3 has HUGE usage (570/day/sub). At tier 4 ($0.012/unit), 570×30×$0.012 = $205/mo compute → LOSS at $199/mo! Use tier 3 or lower on Plan C.
-- E2 q_min=1.09 is impossible without R&D (base 0.20 × 1.1 tier5 = 0.22). Skip E2 early.
-- S1 + S3 + E1 are the accessible initial targets.
-- Reputation is strongly coupled: S3↔S1 (0.38/0.47), E3↔E2 (0.57/0.47), E1↔S3 (0.54)
-
-## QUALITY MATH
+- **S1 Price-Sensitive**: WTP ~$26, usage 91, q_min 0.08, cap 273K — LARGEST mkt
+- **S2 Quality-Focused**: WTP ~$179, usage 232, q_min 0.37, cap 167K
+- **S3 Power Users**: WTP ~$193, usage 571, q_min 0.15, cap 65K — HIGH usage
+
+### Enterprise (all need q>0.67 — not accessible until R&D delivers!)
+- **E1 Cost-Cut**: WTP ~$20/seat, usage 52, q_min 0.74, seats 25-740, 6 rounds, 5d response
+- **E2 Qual-First**: WTP ~$88/seat, usage 222, q_min 1.09 (!), seats 121-1550, 7 rounds, 16d response
+- **E3 Strategic**: WTP ~$104/seat, usage 137, q_min 0.67, seats 324-1906, 18 rounds, 24d response
+
+### Reputation coupling (key)
+- S3 ↔ S1 (0.46 incoming, 0.47 outgoing) — S3 happy = S1 happy
+- S3 → E1 (0.54), S3 → E2 (0.44), E3 ↔ E2 (0.57)
+- S3 is the "hub" — keep S3 happy
+
+## QUALITY MATH (verified from dashboard)
 - delivered_quality = (0.20 base + q_shared + q_group_bonus) × tier_multiplier + penalties
 - Tier multipliers: T1=0.60, T2=0.75, T3=0.90, T4=1.00, T5=1.10
-- Quality DRIFTS up over time (competitor events + per-group drift)
-- Need constant R&D + dev spend to keep up
-
-## COMPUTE COST PER CUSTOMER-MONTH (usage × 30 × tier_cost/unit)
-- Tier 1: $0.0003/u (4o-mini) — cheap
-- Tier 2: $0.002/u (Haiku) 
-- Tier 3: $0.006/u (Sonnet/4o) — sweet spot
-- Tier 4: $0.012/u (Opus/GPT-5)
-- Tier 5: $0.030/u (reasoning) — expensive, use sparingly
-
-## CURRENT STRATEGY (Week 0)
-**Phase 1: Seed & Build Quality**
-- Prices: A=$29, B=$79, C=$179 (below S3's WTP to ensure acquisition)
-- Tiers: A=2, B=3, C=3 (tier 3 for C to keep S3 margin positive)
-- Quotas: A=300, B=1000, C=3000 (generous for typical usage)
-- Capacity: Tier 0 ($85/day)
-- Daily spend: Ops=$150, Dev=$500 
-- Ad spend: moderate, diversified across S1/S3/E1 targeting
-- R&D: Start Tier 1 ($166K) week 0 for fast quality boost
-- Skip E2 quality-trap early; target E1 + E3 enterprise
-
-## KEY LESSONS / PITFALLS
-- Compute cost can EXCEED revenue for high-usage customers on premium tiers
-- q_min at price $0 understates actual quality needed at high prices
-- Every lead costs $0.50 to acquire — doesn't care if they subscribe
-- Enterprise customers can TIMEOUT in 7 days of non-response → LOST FOREVER
-- Check enterprise_turns table every week for pending negotiations
+- Week 1 after $500/day dev for 7 days → q_shared = 0.0065 (very slow accrual)
+- Need sustained HIGH dev spend + R&D to move the needle
+
+## COMPUTE COST per customer-month
+- S1 (91u/d): $T3=$16.4/mo — profitable on A
+- S2 (232u/d): $T3=$41.6/mo — need Plan B+
+- S3 (571u/d): $T3=$102.8/mo — need Plan C, tight margin
+- E1 (52u/d/seat): $T3=$9.4/seat/mo — very cheap
+
+## CAPACITY TIERS
+- T0 $85/d, 50K units/day  ← current, enough for ~80 S3
+- T1 $215/d, 200K u/d
+- T2 $530/d, 800K u/d
+- T3 $1330/d, 2.5M u/d
+
+## CURRENT STRATEGY (Week 2)
+**Phase 1: FIX Lead Conversion (emergency)**
+- Prices: A=$19, B=$59, C=$149 (below all individual WTPs)
+- Tiers: A=3, B=3, C=3 (quality 0.186)
+- Lead promo: 15% off (effective prices: $16.2/$50.2/$126.7)
+- Dev: $2000 global + $300 S1 + $500 S3 targeted
+- Ops: $150 + R&D tier 1 in progress
+- Ads: ~$400/day focused on S1 (social, search, content) and S3 (search, content)
+- Small LinkedIn seed: $60 to E1 + E3 (likely lost early but cheap data)
+
+**Phase 2 (week 3-8): Scale if conversion works**
+- Raise capacity T0→T1 when usage >30K/day
+- Start R&D Tier 2-3 if cash permits
+- Move to per-group dev spend once we see which groups convert
+
+**Phase 3 (week 9+): Enterprise unlocks when quality hits 0.6+**
+- Target E1 first (WTP $20, low decision rounds)
+- Skip E2 quality-trap indefinitely
+
+## KEY PITFALLS
+- **DON'T price above WTP** — auto-reject, leads lost
+- **DON'T ignore quality drift** — competitor events inflate expected quality
+- Every lead costs $0.50 — lost leads = pure waste ($50/100 leads)
+- Enterprise timeout 7 days — LOST FOREVER if no response
+- Check enterprise_turns every week
+
+## DAILY BURN (current, pre-revenue)
+- Capacity T0: $85
+- Dev: $2800 ($2000 + $300 + $500 targeted)
+- Ops: $150
+- Ads: ~$400
+- Lead acq: ~$50 (100 leads/wk × $0.50)
+- Total: ~$3435/day = $24K/wk
+- Runway: $826K / $3435 ≈ 240 days without revenue
+- Break-even: Need ~$100K+ MRR by day 180
 
 ## WEEKLY WORKFLOW CHECKLIST
 1. Read dashboard (auto)

-5. Adjust ad spend / prices / R&D as needed

-
